title: teucrium viscidum var. viscidum
intro: Teucrium viscidum var. viscidum, also known as glandular wood mint or sticky germander, is a plant species within the family Lamiaceae, characterized by its sticky leaves and small white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Teucrium viscidum var. viscidum include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and provide relief from respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. Teucrium viscidum var. viscidum can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, making it a popular ingredient in various herbal remedies and traditional medicine practices.
